2020-07-01.abstract: Protesters deleted social media accounts, as formerly allowed speech suddenly became a potential crime. The chill over the city has booksellers, professors and nonprofits questioning their future.

The Supreme Court granted Alabama’s request to restore voting restrictions during the pandemic. China is downplaying expectations ahead of a planned trip to the country by W.H.O. officials.

2020-07-24.abstract: The party accused Ren Zhiqiang, an influential property tycoon who criticized Xi Jinping, of being disloyal, took aim at his children and paved the way for criminal prosecution.

2020-07-24.title: Divisions, Decency and ‘The Plague’
2020-07-24.abstract: What should we do about the uneven distribution of pandemic consequences?

2020-07-24.title: Refugee and Author Long Detained by Australia Gets Asylum in New Zealand
2020-07-24.abstract: Behrouz Boochani, a Kurdish-Iranian exile, said the news showed the vast differences between the two neighboring countries on human rights.

2020-07-24.title: Thailand Drops All Charges Against Red Bull Heir in Deadly Crash
2020-07-24.abstract: Vorayuth Yoovidhaya, grandson of the energy drinks inventor, had fled the country after striking a police officer with his car in Bangkok in 2012.

2020-07-25.title: The Coronavirus Unleashed Along the Amazon River
2020-07-25.abstract: As the pandemic assails Brazil, the virus is taking an exceptionally high toll on the Amazon region.

2020-07-25.title: Mothers’ Power in U.S. Protests Echoes a Global Tradition
2020-07-25.abstract: When mothers take to the streets — particularly those from privileged groups — governments take note. The “wall of moms” in Portland has taken up the cause against police violence.

2020-07-25.title: These Monkeys Were Once Revered. Now They Are Taking Over.
2020-07-25.abstract: The monkeys of Lopburi, Thailand, were once a draw for tourists and pilgrims who would feed them. But with few recent visitors, the monkeys are getting hungry — and aggressive.

2020-07-25.title: Vietnam, Lauded in Coronavirus Fight, Has First Local Case in 100 Days
2020-07-25.abstract: A 57-year-old grandfather in the city of Danang, with no history of travel, tested positive and has been hospitalized. Health officials are tracing his contacts.

2020-07-25.title: Protests Swell in Russia’s Far East in a Stark New Challenge to Putin
2020-07-25.abstract: Demonstrations in the city of Khabarovsk drew tens of thousands for the third straight weekend. The anger, fueled by the arrest of a popular governor, has little precedent in modern Russia.

2020-07-26.title: Testing Results Take Too Long, U.S. Official Concedes
2020-07-26.abstract: Florida has now recorded more cases than New York. Britain orders travelers from Spain into 14 days of isolation, upending vacation plans for thousands.

2020-07-26.title: Strange Bedfellows: Iran’s Ex-President Woos Saudi Crown Prince as Pen Pal
2020-07-26.abstract: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, known in the West for his Holocaust denial and disputed re-election in 2009, writes a warm letter to Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, calling him a man of peace.

2020-07-26.title: Volunteer Confesses to Starting Fire at Nantes Cathedral
2020-07-26.abstract: French prosecutors have charged the man, a Rwandan refugee, with arson. He had been questioned and released earlier in the investigation.

2020-07-26.title: Fighting False News in Ukraine, Facebook Fact Checkers Tread a Blurry Line
2020-07-26.abstract: Facebook hired a Ukrainian group battling Russian disinformation to flag misleading posts. But critics say the fact checkers’ work veers into activism.

2020-07-26.title: Quarantine Order Blindsides Britons Returning From Spain
2020-07-26.abstract: Britain’s sudden decision to make travelers from Spain self-isolate for 14 days caught many vacationers by surprise — including the transportation secretary.

2020-07-27.title: South Korea Confirms a Defector Swam Back to the North
2020-07-27.abstract: North Korea has accused him of bringing Covid-19 across the border. A South Korean news outlet says the 24-year-old man was wanted for questioning over a rape accusation.

2020-07-27.title: Research Boosts Evidence of Masks’ Utility, Some Experts Say
2020-07-27.abstract: Masks have long been known to help stop infected people from spreading the virus, but some research suggests they also protect the uninfected. President Trump shared a video with misinformation about the virus.

2020-07-27.title: An American Mustache That Irritated South Koreans Is No More
2020-07-27.abstract: Blaming the summer’s heat, the U.S. ambassador lopped off a bit of facial hair that had reminded some of brutal colonial Japan.

2020-07-27.title: Boris Johnson’s New Tactic Against the Virus: Urge Britons to Lose Weight
2020-07-27.abstract: The prime minister, who cited his own problems with obesity, said his hospitalization with Covid-19 had prompted his push to tighten regulations on junk food.

2020-07-27.title: Israel Says It Thwarted a Hezbollah Raid at Lebanon Border
2020-07-27.abstract: The Israeli military says it repelled a raid by Hezbollah in a disputed area along its northern border. Hezbollah denied carrying out an operation. No casualties were reported.

2020-07-28.title: Najib Razak, Malaysia’s Former Prime Minister, Found Guilty in Graft Trial
2020-07-28.abstract: Mr. Najib was sentenced to up to 12 years in prison and faces fines of nearly $50 million on charges of abuse of power, breach of trust and money laundering.

2020-07-28.title: Australia Says Chinese Students Are Targets in ‘Virtual Kidnapping’ Scams
2020-07-28.abstract: Recent cases reveal the evolution of a crime that often exploits worry over family members abroad with digital savvy and old-fashioned coercion.

2020-07-28.title: In Russia’s Far East, a New Face of Resistance to Putin’s Reign
2020-07-28.abstract: As the protests swell in the city of Khabarovsk, 4,000 miles from Moscow, residents who had never before found a public outlet for anger are becoming activists.

2020-07-28.title: Trump Falsely Claims Much of U.S. Is ‘Corona-Free’
2020-07-28.abstract: A national teachers’ union said teachers might strike as a “last resort” if they didn’t feel safe. Pilgrims to Mecca are finding a reconfigured hajj.

2020-07-28.title: British-Australian Academic Jailed in Iran Is Moved to Remote Prison
2020-07-28.abstract: Kylie Moore-Gilbert, who has denied charges of espionage, is now in a facility where many have been infected with the coronavirus, rights activists say.

2020-07-29.title: Latin America Is Facing a ‘Decline of Democracy’ Under the Pandemic
2020-07-29.abstract: The coronavirus is battering Latin American health systems and economies. It is also threatening the region’s fragile political freedoms.

2020-07-29.title: Turkey Passes Law Extending Sweeping Powers Over Social Media
2020-07-29.abstract: The legislation extends control over platforms like Facebook, Twitter and YouTube. Critics worry it will be used to stifle dissent and criticism of the government.

2020-07-29.title: Judge Blocks Green Card Wealth Tests, Citing Pandemic Hardships
2020-07-29.abstract: Deaths top 150,000 in the United States. A $600 unemployment benefit is likely to lapse on Friday, a top official said. In Iran, religious ceremonies and college-entrance exams are in limbo.

2020-07-29.title: For Senegal’s Biggest Holiday, a Shortage of the All-Important Sheep
2020-07-29.abstract: Properly celebrating Tabaski, as Eid al-Adha is known in Senegal, requires a sacrificial sheep. Coronavirus restrictions have made the animals more expensive, putting them out of reach of many.

2020-07-29.title: Mysterious Coronavirus Outbreak Catches Vietnam by Surprise
2020-07-29.abstract: After months without a single coronavirus death, or even a confirmed case of local transmission, a new outbreak has struck Vietnam. And it’s spreading.

2020-07-30.title: One in Three Children Have Unacceptably High Lead Levels, Study Says
2020-07-30.abstract: “Children around the world are being poisoned by lead on a massive and previously unrecognized scale,” according to the study, a collaboration of UNICEF and Pure Earth, an advocacy group.

2020-07-30.title: Hong Kong Is Keeping Pro-Democracy Candidates Out of Its Election
2020-07-30.abstract: Twelve candidates, including several prominent democracy advocates, were barred from an upcoming legislative election, and four activists were arrested over online posts.

2020-07-30.title: A Cool Trudeau Rebuffs Conflict Charges. Will Canadians Buy His Story?
2020-07-30.abstract: There was no corruption, just a government working to save lives during a pandemic. That’s the message the prime minister delivered to skeptical lawmakers, and to Canadians.

2020-07-30.title: The Dictator Who Waged War on Darfur Is Gone, but the Killing Goes On
2020-07-30.abstract: The ouster of Sudan’s longtime ruler last year stoked hopes for peace in brutalized Darfur. But violence has surged amid fresh accusations against Sudan’s security forces.

2020-07-30.title: Fauci to Testify Before Congress on Coronavirus Response
2020-07-30.abstract: Federal U.S. jobless benefits are set to expire after lawmakers failed to make a deal. Herman Cain died after being hospitalized with the virus.

2020-07-31.title: What Can Victorian Schools Teach America About Reopening?
2020-07-31.abstract: With around 100 schools closed because of students or teachers with COVID-19, Australia has lessons for other countries aiming to reopen.

2020-07-31.title: Hope, Despair, Control: The 1950s China My Father Saw, Echoed Today
2020-07-31.abstract: William Stevenson was one of the first foreign correspondents to visit the People’s Republic of China. Decades later, despite its transformation, I recognize the same country.

2020-07-31.title: In Indonesia, False Virus Cures Pushed by Those Who Should Know Better
2020-07-31.abstract: In the absence of a unified message from the national government, local officials and opportunists have filled the gap with misinformation and quack remedies.

2020-07-31.title: U.K. Officials’ New Trump Dilemma: What if He Loses?
2020-07-31.abstract: If Joe Biden wins the November election, Britain would face a president who opposed Brexit, would look out for Ireland, and may have little interest in a trade deal.

2020-07-31.title: A $600-a-Week Lifeline for Unemployed Americans Expires After an Impasse in Washington
2020-07-31.abstract: California became the first state to reach 500,000 total coronavirus cases. Once the site of a major outbreak, Italy now offers lessons for keeping the virus in check.
